description_long: This object consists of a stack of rectangular reddish-brown stone blocks arranged in a low wall formation, with several loose blocks scattered in front and to the sides. The blocks have a rough, weathered texture, with visible chips and irregularities along the edges, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The coloration varies subtly between blocks, ranging from deep red to brown, with some darker and lighter patches. The stacked section forms a partial barrier, while the loose blocks indicate recent disturbance or ongoing construction. The surface of each block is matte and uneven, with a granular, stone-like appearance. The overall structure is solid and heavy, typical of materials used for urban barriers or low retaining walls.
description: A stack of reddish-brown, rough-textured stone blocks forming a partial wall, with several loose blocks scattered nearby. The blocks show signs of weathering and have a solid, heavy appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stacked wall of stone blocks with several loose blocks scattered in front.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stacked blocks and the scattered loose blocks extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view of the stacked wall, with fewer loose blocks visible and the alignment of the blocks more apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness of the wall and the arrangement of loose blocks.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or roadside construction barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: The blocks display chipped corners and rough surfaces, with some discoloration indicating exposure to weather. No significant moss or biological growth is visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall with loosely scattered blocks
